sentences_input.text: And then there were any number of smaller synagogues, you-- maintained by some private people with means who lived, perhaps, a little too far out so that they could not come during the Sabbath or, or holidays easily by foot and return home to any one of the other synagogues; or who, for a variety of [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] personal needs and conveniences, felt that they had to have a prayer house literally in their house or very nearly to it.

[PAUSES FOR 4 SECONDS] The educational system-- that really should be called the Jewish system was a, a kind of a four-tiered system where you had the cheders, which youngsters began to attend beginning from age three.

I remember when my time came, and my hair was cut, leaving only the payos, at age three at-- with the appropriate celebration, being taken to the cheder that was attached to where Moshe Klein's home, which, in effect, was what might be called pre-kindergarten or, or kindergarten, and where at age three, you were-- began to be taught the Hebrew alphabet and, and reading.

[PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] And then you had various levels as you advanced in age and knowledge, until you were right for what they called the Talmud Torah, which might be called sort of the middle school.
[PAUSES FOR 5 SECONDS] This was-- the, the earlier grades were largely private rabbis or teachers who maintained their own institutions from the fees that the students paid.
